public_reputation: Evaluator who determines magical artifact value, danger, and admissibility within protected academic channels.
appearance: Relic Appraiser Thene Voss carries the marks of relic appraiser in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Carries the habits of a relic appraiser whose work is visible through daily procedure, gatekeeping, and the practical pressures of Arcane Aegis.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of relic appraiser in and around Arcane Aegis, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Arcane Aegis while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Relic Appraiser Thene Voss can draw the party into a practical problem centered on Arcane Aegis.
Relic Appraiser Thene Voss becomes useful when the party needs access, explanation, or relief tied to the work of a relic appraiser.

support_scope_notes: Created from the estate and daily-life infrastructure execution patch to make polity-native estate operations usable at table scale.
public_signal: In public, Relic Appraiser Thene Voss is readable as a relic appraiser tied to Arcane Aegis.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Arcane Aegis: role-relevant halls, counters, corridors, docks, archives, shrines, workshops, gates, or administrative rooms

first_impression: Relic Appraiser Thene Voss presents as someone shaped by the duties of a relic appraiser and the daily pressures of Arcane Aegis.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a relic appraiser, and proof that strangers will not make Arcane Aegis harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Relic Appraiser Thene Voss refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a relic appraiser too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Relic Appraiser Thene Voss can open local doors, explain expectations, and redirect pressure before it hardens.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Relic Appraiser Thene Voss adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Relic Appraiser Thene Voss always knows more about Arcane Aegis than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to relic appraiser duties in Arcane Aegis.
social_texture: Relic Appraiser Thene Voss speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Arcane Aegis, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Relic touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Relic Appraiser Thene Voss when the party needs a socially grounded face for Arcane Aegis: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
